title: LDAP
description:
Workato's LDAP connector is an universal connector that can be used to connect to any LDAP service.

### Prerequisites:

1. [OPA extension](https://workato-opa-extensions.s3.amazonaws.com/ldap-extensions/ldap-extension-latest.zip) along with dependent Apache LDAP API client (https://directory.apache.org/api/download-old-versions.html)

2. Config.yml in config folder in OPA installation directory has below config:

```
server:
     classpath: /usr/lib/workato-agent/ext 
 extensions:
   ldap:
     controllerClass: com.workato.extension.ldap.ExtensionController
```

### The OPA extension is tested with below dependencies
- api-all 2.1.6
- caffeine-2.9.3
- mina-core-2.2.3

### TRIGGERS (0)
- No triggers defined
### ACTIONS (1)
- ### Search records
  Returns all records that matches your search criteria.

## Support

This connector was built and is supported by Workato. Send all your bug reports through our support channels, found [here](https://docs.workato.com/contact-us.html#for-reporting-bugs). Let our support team know the name of this connector and that it was installed from the community so they can route your support tickets properly. To request enhancements, use the following [link](https://docs.workato.com/contact-us.html#for-requesting-enhancements) and select your region’s data center.
note:
Disable automatic schema discovery for search action

- Changed `extends_schema` to `false` in `search_object_input`
- Bypasses RootDSE query for `schemaNamingContext` to avoid 403/404 errors
- Enables manual JSON schema definition for restricted LDAP environments
- Resolves: "Attribute schemaNamingContext not found" error

by - shrejosh@redhat.com
